title: Star Wars 3-D 2

|timeline=[[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]]<ref name="3-D">''[[Star Wars 3-D]]'' presents a number of continuity issues in regards to the [[Evacuation of Yavin]] and the establishment of [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]], conflicting with all other depictions of those events. [[Star Wars 3-D 1|''Star Wars 3-D'' 1]] must occur before the Evacuation, while [[Star Wars 3-D 2|''Star Wars 3-D'' 2]] and [[Star Wars 3-D 3|3]] are set in its direct aftermath, though it depicts the main cast as absent from Yavin during the evacuation. Thus, it must be set in approximately 0.5 ABY per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref>
|series=''[[Star Wars 3-D]]''
|issue=2
}}
'''''Star Wars 3-D'' 2''' is the second issue of the ''[[Star Wars 3-D]]'' series of comics.

==Plot summary==
Immediately following their adventures on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] return to the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]] in the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. However, they find the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion's]] base on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] surrounded by an [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Fleet]] led by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Quist (admiral)|Quist]] and headed by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. Han is able to pull off some fancy flying to escape and land on the world while Quist is subsequently executed by Vader for his failure. Arriving at an old [[Great Temple/Legends|Yavin Base]], Luke and the rest assume the Rebels have fled, due to it being abandoned. Luckily, the group is able to catch the fleet at a secret rendezvous where Princess Leia informs the group that they need to find a new base. Han suggests the ice world of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], a world which the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] is sure to overlook. The group leave to scout the unfamiliar territory, and after arriving, they use their [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] to search for a large ice cavern to serve as a suitable base. Unfortunately, the search leads to an encounter with the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Salmakk]] who tries to claim the bounty on Han's head. Fleeing in their landspeeder, the group accidentally flies right into the smuggler's headquarters. Luke uses his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] to cut stalactites off of the ceiling of the base causing the cave to begin collapsing on itself. The Rebels are able to escape just in time as Salmakk and his cohorts are trapped under the rubble. Luke and Han are happy to have escaped, but realize that they still have to find a base for the displaced Rebellion.

==Continuity==
The events in this comic take place after the many [[Battle of Yavin 4|Battles of Yavin 4]], and the Rebels evacuation from their Yavin 4 base. However, unlike in other sources, this comic depicts Luke, Han, C-3PO, and Chewbacca away from the base as it is being evacuated. Additionally the exploration and selection of Hoth as the next base in this comic is somewhat contrary to the world's discovery in [[Classic Star Wars]] though it is possible that the two stories coincide somehow.

It's interesting to note that both Salmakk and Han Solo refer to [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]] as Jabba the ''Hut'', a misspelling that was prevalent in the [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel Star Wars]] comic line, where Jabba was portrayed as a [[Nimbanel/Legends|Nimbanel]] that some fans believe to be Jabba's accountant, [[Mosep Binneed/Legends|Mosep Binneed]].
